name: Empty course landing page
rationale: Your course is empty. Start with a page that welcomes students and explains what the course is about.

name: Pre-start with no content
rationale: The course hasn't started yet and has no materials. Add an introductory page outlining its aims and structure.

name: Pre-start introductions forum
rationale: Give students a place to introduce themselves before teaching begins.

name: Activities but no content
rationale: You have activities but no explanatory material. Add a page so students know what to do.

name: Week 1 icebreaker poll
condition: term_stage == week1 AND module_count.choice == 0 AND has_purpose_communication == false
rationale: A quick poll in week one is a low-effort way to get every student participating early.

name: Crowded single section
rationale: Several activities sit in one section. A contents page helps students navigate.

name: Pre-start syllabus book
condition: term_stage == prestart AND module_count.page < 1 AND module_count.book < 1
rationale: Use a book to lay out a multi-page syllabus before the course opens.

name: Resources without orientation
rationale: Add a short label at the top of section one to orient students to the materials.

name: Undated self-paced course
rationale: This course has no start or end date. Add a page noting expected pacing for self-paced learners.

name: All passive no interaction
rationale: Everything here is read-only. Add an interactive H5P element so students engage, not just read.

name: Freshly rolled over
condition: course_age_days < 14 AND has_purpose_content == true AND recent_events_7d < 5
rationale: This looks like a freshly copied course. A quick choice confirms students can access the materials.

name: Many loose files
rationale: You have many separate files. Group related ones into a folder to reduce clutter.

name: Content but no assessment
rationale: Students have plenty to read but nothing to check their understanding. A short quiz closes that loop.

name: Heavy reading no deep task
condition: has_purpose_content == true AND has_purpose_assessment == false AND module_count.book > 0
rationale: Your reading material is substantial. An assignment lets students demonstrate deeper understanding.

name: No communication channel
rationale: There is no way for students to ask questions or discuss. Add a forum for course conversation.

name: Large cohort no discussion
rationale: With a large class, a discussion forum cuts repeated questions and builds community.

name: No collaboration activity
rationale: Students only ever work alone here. A wiki lets them build something together.

name: Groups set but unused
rationale: Groups are configured but no activity uses them. A group wiki puts those groups to work.

name: Term-heavy no glossary
rationale: A glossary gives students a shared reference for key terms as they read.

name: No shared data collection
rationale: A database activity lets the class collect and share examples, references, or fieldwork.

name: Quiz-only assessment
rationale: Assessment is all quiz-based. An assignment adds an authentic task quizzes can't capture.

name: Assignment-only assessment
rationale: All assessment is assignment-based. Short quizzes give faster, low-stakes feedback.

name: Lecture pages no check
rationale: Several pages of material but no formative check. A quiz after the reading aids retention.

name: Single high-stakes item
rationale: There is only one graded item. A low-stakes practice quiz reduces pressure on the single assessment.

name: No feedback instrument
rationale: There is no way to gather student opinion. A feedback activity surfaces what is and isn't working.

name: Linear content branching
rationale: With this much linear content, a lesson can guide students adaptively based on their answers.

name: Writing tasks no peer review
rationale: Multiple writing tasks but no peer review. A workshop lets students learn by assessing each other.

name: No quick decision tool
rationale: A choice activity is the fastest way to take a class decision or quick pulse.

name: No interactive content
rationale: Add interactive content so students practise actively rather than only consuming.

name: Only one activity type
rationale: The whole course uses a single activity type. Variety keeps students engaged; start with a forum.

name: No content pages at all
rationale: There are no explanatory pages. Add one to frame the activities students see.

name: Text-only variety
rationale: Link out to a relevant video or article to add variety to mostly text-based material.

name: No reflection late
condition: module_count.assign > 0 AND has_feedback_activity == false AND term_stage in (mid|late)
rationale: Add a short reflection so students think about their own progress, not just receive a grade.

name: Completion on nothing tracked
rationale: Completion is on but nothing is tracked. A quiz with completion gives students a sense of progress.

name: Groups without shared space
condition: has_groups == true AND module_count.forum == 0 AND module_count.wiki == 0
rationale: Groups exist but have nowhere to talk. A group forum gives each team its own space.

name: Big-class FAQ
rationale: Recurring questions in a big class are easier to manage as a searchable FAQ glossary.

name: No summative path
rationale: The course is well underway with no graded work. Add an assignment so learning can be assessed.

name: Content-rich not interactive
condition: has_purpose_content == true AND has_purpose_interactivecontent == false AND activity_count > 8
rationale: A content-rich course benefits from interactive H5P to break up long passive stretches.

name: Large grouped cohort coordination
condition: module_count.chat == 0 AND enrolled_students > 30 AND has_groups == true
rationale: For a large, grouped cohort, a forum scales better than scattered emails for coordination.

name: Single-activity shell unset
rationale: This single-activity course has no activity yet. Choose the activity it should present.

name: Week 1 diagnostic
rationale: A short diagnostic in week one shows what students already know before you teach.

name: Early formative quiz
rationale: Early term is the right time for a low-stakes quiz so students get used to being assessed.

name: Mid-course feedback
rationale: You're halfway through. A mid-course feedback activity lets you adjust before it's too late.

name: Mid-course collaboration
rationale: Mid-term is a good moment to introduce collaborative work now students know each other.

name: Late summative quiz
rationale: As the course nears its end, a summative quiz helps students consolidate the whole syllabus.

name: Late no major task
rationale: There's no major assessed task near the end. Add a final assignment to capture overall learning.

name: End-of-course feedback
rationale: Collect end-of-course feedback while the experience is fresh so the next run is better.

name: Post-end reflection
rationale: The course has finished. A short feedback activity captures what students valued most.

name: Peer review window open
condition: term_stage in (early|mid) AND module_count.workshop == 0 AND module_count.assign > 0
rationale: There's still time to run a peer review. Workshops need a few weeks, so set one up now.

name: Week 1 goals
rationale: Ask students about their goals or availability in week one with a quick choice.

name: Early reading checks
condition: term_stage in (week1|early) AND module_count.page > 2 AND module_count.quiz == 0
rationale: Students have reading to do early on. A quick check keeps them honest and on pace.

name: Mid-course Q&A
rationale: Questions pile up mid-term. Open a Q&A forum so they're answered once, publicly.

name: Pre-finals revision glossary
rationale: A glossary built before finals gives students a single revision reference for key terms.

name: Pre-start expectations
rationale: Before the course opens, set expectations on workload and assessment on a clear page.

name: Early community forum
rationale: Community forms early or not at all. Add a social forum in the first weeks.

name: Mid progress marker
condition: term_stage == mid AND enablecompletion == true AND completion_tracked_count < 3
rationale: Halfway through with little completion tracking. A tracked quiz gives a visible progress marker.

name: Late interactive revision
rationale: Interactive revision suits the run-up to assessment better than re-reading notes.

name: Self-paced pacing aid
condition: term_stage == undated AND module_count.lesson == 0 AND module_count.page > 4
rationale: For a self-paced course, a lesson paces content and checks understanding as students go.

name: Week 1 introductions
rationale: An introductions forum in week one helps a cohort feel like a group.

name: Mid-course temperature check
rationale: A one-click poll mid-term is an easy temperature check on how the course is landing.

name: Late feedback not peer review
condition: term_stage == late AND module_count.assign > 0 AND module_count.feedback == 0
rationale: Too late for new peer review, but a quick feedback form still captures end-stage opinion.

name: Pre-course goals survey
rationale: Find out what students want from the course before you're too far in to adjust.

name: Mid class-built data
condition: term_stage == mid AND module_count.data == 0 AND enrolled_students > 15
rationale: Mid-course is ideal for a class-built database of cases or examples to study from.

name: Late consolidation book
condition: term_stage == late AND module_count.book == 0 AND module_count.page > 5
rationale: Pull scattered late-term pages into a single book students can revise from.

name: Post-end closing page
rationale: Add a closing page thanking students and pointing to next steps or follow-on courses.

name: Early self-grouping
condition: term_stage in (week1|early) AND has_groups == false AND enrolled_students > 20
rationale: Use a choice to let students self-select into groups or topics early on.

name: Dead passive content
rationale: Several resources have never been opened. Swap one for an interactive H5P that invites action.

name: Quiet week
rationale: The course has been quiet this week. A forum prompt is a gentle way to draw students back.

name: Low completion rate
rationale: Completion is low. A quick, easy choice can rebuild momentum with an early win.

name: Silent forum
rationale: Your forum is quiet. Sometimes a single poll sparks the discussion a blank forum can't.

name: Quizzes ignored
condition: module_count.quiz > 0 AND quiz_attempt_count < enrolled_students AND term_stage in (early|mid)
rationale: Few students have attempted the quiz. A choice asking what's blocking them can reveal why.

name: Low submission rate
rationale: Submissions are low. A support forum lets struggling students ask for help before the deadline.

name: Enrolled but inactive
rationale: Many enrolled but few active. A short feedback form can uncover why students dropped off.

name: New material unseen
rationale: New material may be going unnoticed. A choice nudges students to confirm they've seen it.

name: Stalled mid-course
condition: term_stage == mid AND recent_events_7d < 15 AND enrolled_students > 15
rationale: Activity has stalled at mid-term. Ask students directly what would help them re-engage.

name: Graded work avoided
rationale: Graded work isn't being completed. A short, low-stakes quiz can ease students back in.

name: Glossary unused
rationale: If the glossary isn't being used, a term-of-the-week choice can drive students to it.

name: One-way communication
rationale: Discussion is one-directional. A poll gives quieter students an easy way to take part.

name: Narrow and quiet
rationale: Limited variety may be dampening engagement. An interactive activity adds a different hook.

name: Early drop-off
condition: course_age_days > 21 AND active_students_7d < enrolled_students AND term_stage == early
rationale: Early drop-off is common but recoverable. A welcoming forum prompt can bring students back.

name: Possible assessment anxiety
condition: module_count.quiz > 0 AND quiz_attempt_count < enrolled_students AND module_count.feedback == 0
rationale: Low quiz uptake can signal anxiety. A quick anonymous feedback form helps you understand it.
preconfig: name=How are you finding the quizzes?

name: Sustain good progress
rationale: Most students are progressing well. A light choice can celebrate and sustain momentum.

name: Inactive wiki
rationale: If the wiki has gone quiet, a forum thread can coordinate who works on what next.

name: Pre-finals dip
rationale: Engagement often dips before finals. A revision quiz gives students a reason to return.

name: Interactive content skipped
rationale: If interactive content is being skipped, a choice pointing students to it can help.

name: Low feedback response
rationale: Feedback response is low. A single-question choice often gets answers a long form won't.

name: No early quick win
rationale: Give students an early, easy completion. A simple choice builds the habit of finishing tasks.

name: Passive-heavy and quiet
rationale: A mostly passive course that's gone quiet benefits from an active task students must do.

name: Large silent class
rationale: In a big, quiet class, a poll lowers the bar to participation more than a forum.

name: Mid-course plateau
condition: completion_rate > 40 AND completion_rate < 70 AND term_stage == mid
rationale: Progress has plateaued mid-course. A fresh quiz can re-motivate students stuck in the middle.

name: No external perspective
rationale: Linking to an external resource or expert adds a perspective beyond your own materials.

name: Document-heavy no video
rationale: Mostly documents here. A linked video suits students who learn better by watching.

name: Many pages auto-glossary
rationale: An auto-linking glossary turns key terms across your many pages into instant definitions.

name: Loose pages into book
rationale: Eight or more loose pages are easier to follow gathered into a single navigable book.

name: Mature toolset add specialist
rationale: You use many core tools well. An external tool can add a specialist activity Moodle lacks.

name: Prefer H5P to SCORM
rationale: Prefer H5P over SCORM for new interactive content; it's easier to author and maintain.

name: Large cohort structured survey
rationale: For a large cohort, a structured feedback activity gives analysable, comparable responses.

name: Polls without depth
rationale: You use quick polls a lot. A feedback activity captures the richer, open responses polls miss.

name: Assessment variety low
rationale: Plenty of assessment but little variety. A workshop adds peer assessment to the mix.

name: Well-rounded stretch
condition: has_purpose_assessment == true AND has_purpose_communication == true AND has_purpose_collaboration == true AND has_purpose_interactivecontent == false
rationale: This course is well-balanced. The one gap is interactive content; H5P would round it out.
